backgroundInformationList.description: Notice of Intention to Remove a Holding Symbol (H) from Zoning By-law 265-2024 - 1571 Sandhurst Circle

noticeDescription:
NOTICE OF INTENTION TO REMOVE A HOLDING SYMBOL (H) FROM ZONING BY-LAW 265-2024

(Under the Planning Act)

 

The City has received the following application under Section 36 of the Planning Act:

 

Application Number: 24 142261 ESC 23 OZ

Address: 1571 Sandhurst Circle

Ward: 23 - Scarborough North

Contact: Laura Dainard, Senior Planner, Community Planning at 416-396-7023, by e-mail at Laura.Dainard@toronto.ca, or by address Scarborough Civic Centre, 150 Borough Drive, 3rd Floor, Scarborough, ON, M1P 4N7

 

DESCRIPTION

 

The application to amend Zoning By-law 265-2024 to remove the holding symbol (H) to permit the development of two mid-rise buildings and six tall buildings. The location of where the holding symbol (H) would be removed is noted on the map below.

 

Detailed information about the proposal, including background information and materials may be made available for review by contacting the Planner noted above or by visiting the City of Toronto’s Application Information Centre.  

 

MAKE YOUR VIEWS KNOWN

 

You may send written comments by mail or email to the Community Planner noted under Contact. Your written comments must be submitted no later than July 3, 2026, to be considered by the Executive Director, Development Review or their designate, Director, Community Planning, Scarborough District. A decision by the Executive Director, Development Review or their designate as to whether to remove the holding symbol (H) will occur no earlier than July 21, 2026.

 

Notice to people writing to the City: The City of Toronto Act, 2006, the Planning Act, and the City of Toronto Municipal Code authorize the City of Toronto to collect any personal information in your communication to the City.

 

The City collects this information to enable it to make informed decisions on the relevant issue(s).  If you are submitting letters, e-mails, or other communications to the City, you should be aware that your name and the fact that you communicated with the City will become part of the public record and will appear on the City's website. The City will also make your communication and any personal information in it - such as your postal address, telephone number or e-mail address - available to the public, unless you expressly request the City to remove it.
